tokens: Jim, when you get out, be very careful you don't fall backwards.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Be careful you don't go backwards, now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm telling you, we have climbed a steep hill, and we didn't even really realize it!
And, we were going like 10 clicks up this hill, and we're on a slope of...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we can look back and see the whole...We can see the LM just as loud and clear as can be.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Gosh, I'll tell you, this Rover is really something!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Joe, when the TV comes on, you're going to get a picture.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we may ask you to dust our TV lens off.
We'll ask you to stand by for a reading on that.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Rover, do you read Houston?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: What did you...We'll stand by for you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we're standing by for the picture.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I have to get the antenna aligned.
It's going to take a little bit here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughing) I don't know why...(Responding to Joe) Yeah.
I don't know why we always end up on slopes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, I want to take a picture upslope, Dave, but I can't.
I can't get the camera pointed up that way.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just do the best you can on that, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You must have the (TV) picture now, or at least the antenna is pointed.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Probably noticing the TV camera motion) They're starting to move it now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, we're going to leave (that is, move away from) the Rover here and that's why I wondered.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And do we have a picture down here!
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, you couldn't, Joe, because the camera is pointed straight down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's a close-up of the Rover wheel.
(Dave and Jim laugh) And, it's still smoking.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's get on with seeing what's here at the front.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's go up first, so we can come downhill (after sampling).
And, there's one of those fresh little craters.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Dave chuckles) I never thought we'd have a problem like this on the Moon - like we do on field trips - trying to maintain our balance.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's a nice little crater, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, it looks to me like the best thing to do would be to scoop the side...scoop the center (of the crater) where the glass is.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, we're a long way from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: At least, we can see it!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) We never did remark on that very white crater out there northwest of the LM, did we?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I don't think we did.
It's really, really white though, isn't it?
Okay, I've got your bag, and it's number 16(3).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and we're going to sample the glass in the middle of it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Start with the middle, and we'll pick up the rim, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See how it's all kind of welded together.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I hope it stays together for us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And is that still bag number 163?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The next one coming up is 164.
(To Jim) And, why don't you scoop the rim there, Jim.
(Pause as Jim pours the first scoopful of rim soil)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, let's get a good bag full.
(To Joe, while Jim gets a second scoopful of soil) Okay, Joe.
It's very fine, light gray; the rim is.
Can you hold this one, and I'll "Z" the other one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: TV coming in good now, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're trying to look into the Sun at the moment, somewhat unsuccessfully.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You ought to look up toward...You ought to look up toward Mount Hadley.
You can see that linear pattern.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll take a look, and thanks for the recommendation.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's find ourselves a couple of frags down here.
(Pause) Here's a...There are three within easy range over here.
(Pause) Frags show up pretty good down-Sun, don't they?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hate to go after some little ones but...(Pause) Right there in front of you, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And are you still scooping samples?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're sampling a rock right now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, we know you're picking up the representative ones.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Give me a...(Responding to Joe) Yeah.
(Pause) And the number on this bag is 188.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And have you noticed a variety of rock types or just one general kind?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let us go through them, Joe, as we pick them up, because we can't tell any difference as they sit on the surface.
And, the first one here is a fine-grained breccia, a microbreccia.
And, it looks like a third order with white clasts in it.
The matrix is dark black, and it has glass within a fracture on the side.
Not unlike some of the 14s (meaning the Apollo 14 samples)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Want to put some soil in?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Get that other frag right next to it, Jim.
(Pause) And, Joe, the soil is very powdery here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (But we'll collect it) just for drill here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you give me the bag, and why don't you take a little scoop (of soil) right there by the side of the...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I got to get back uphill.
(Pause) I've got most of it, I think.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, there's one upslope with a flat side?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Maybe we could take that back as a large one?
(Pause) Do you want to wait until we get over to a fresh crater?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's wait until we get to a fresh crater.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See if we can get some more typical...Here's one down here to your right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's just make a little circle around the old Rover here and find some variety.
(Long Pause) Get it (probably meaning the down-Sun "befores")?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay; this is a fairly large subangular fragment, which is about 20 percent buried.
I'm not sure we'll get that in the bag!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I do see some glass spattered on one side.
And I also see one little, looks like an orange crystal in there, like it might be a little piece of olivine.
It's got definite reddish-orange color to it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get the ("after") picture before I step in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See if we can get this in a...Oop...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Do you want to try putting it in the bag?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is definitely a different kind of breccia, Joe.
It's only got light-gray millimeter- size clasts in it, with a fine-grained, gray matrix.
And the clasts, there are about, gee, I'd say 10 percent of the total frag.
Here, I can hold it with both hands, if you can stick it in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Just keep going around the old Rover here, and see if we can find another interesting looking one.
(Pause) (To Joe) As you can see, probably, with the TV, Joe, there just isn't much in the way of debris around here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...we might set you out a little later looking for a fresh crater that's brought up some frags for us.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Jim) Jim, there's one sitting on top of this little crater over here.
Reckon you can get over here to it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I was trying to recover that bag, but I gave up on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, we got plenty of bags.
Don't sweat the bags, we got more than we'll ever need.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, on your pan, were you able to sweep around the full 360 degrees?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, you sink in here...(Stops to listen to Joe) Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'll take another one probably before we leave the area, so you get a little stereo effect.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, this is an interesting one, I think.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As you can tell, we sink in about 2 or 3 inches...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, I would say that this fragment here hit right before its position.
See that little spot right there in front?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think that rock hit there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can convince me of that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And it...We'll just have to take a look at it, when we get the ("before") pictures here.
If it did hit there it was traveling...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: East to west, and it left a little mark about a foot from its present position.
And its present position is on the surface.
And we'll pick it up and take a look at it.
As a matter of fact, I'll see if I can't get a close-up of the little spot that it hit here.
Well, if I can lean down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Did you get the down-Sun, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Dave grunts) That stuff (meaning the soil) is really soft.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah; let me get it with the scoop.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) I'll throw it up, and you catch it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) (Switching hands) (I'd better use my) right hand here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Moving to the west side of the rock) Okay.
Let me get down...Let me use my tongs to pick it up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause as he recovers his balance) Well, it's really covered (with dust).
But it's a very rough surface; very sharp; basically a subangular rock, but with quite a jagged, craggy surface on it.
And I can see some spots in there.
I guess I'd just have to call it a breccia.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just let me put it in your bag (meaning Jim's SCB).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I think we have it fairly well documented.
It's in collection bag (SCB) number 3, which will help you keep track of it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: They (meaning the rocks in the area)'re either big ones, or they're little small ones.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's a crater over to the west, Dave, that has a very light albedo that's...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, let's head that way with the Rover when we get going.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...you reach a good stopping point, we've got a couple of questions.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: While you're asking them, I think I'll take another pan.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Dave, while he is doing that, could you tell us how far away and in what direction is the large block which you described?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We intend to head in that direction.
It's probably, oh, 3/10ths of a kilometer or something.
And I think it's on the same slope - maybe upslope a tad - from where we are now, but not too much.
And on the way, there's a nice fresh light-albedo crater, maybe a couple of meters across.
So maybe we ought to pick up those two (that is, stop at both places).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can see, towards the west, a light-albedo fresh crater.
As you look back towards...(Pause) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Do you think this is a good area for a rake sample?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let me swing around and get the down-Sun, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm in a better position, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, do you think that that fresh crater you're looking at might be Spur Crater?
We put your present position as halfway between Window and Spur.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: No, I don't think...It's too small, Joe.
I think we picked up Spur as we went by a little while ago.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We agree with you exactly here, Dave and Jim.
And we want you, when you leave this station, to move back towards the west.
In other words, towards the direction of the rille, and looking especially for fresh craters.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Answering Joe while he examines the sample) Okay, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, a whole...Glass all over the bottom of that one.
And it looks like another microbreccia.
And I don't see any pits in any of these, at all.
I do see a couple of glass...Well, yeah; this one's got a couple of very small glass-filled pits, but most of them are pitless.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Want me to put any soil in it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Answering Jim's question) No, I think we've got enough soil.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I took the down-Sun from a different side on this one...
(Correcting himself) I mean the cross-Sun from a different side on this one, if you want to log that.
You want to stick that in my bag (that is, Dave's SCB) and...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's go down and take a look at this little crater right here.
There's a little small crater, I guess you can see, Joe, at about 2 o'clock to the TV now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, having trouble getting up it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I just got the bag opened.
transcript.speaker: Irwin
